summary: The post assembles firsthand accounts from people in Israel and Gaza to show how the war is devastating daily life, safety, and work across both communities. It argues that beyond the headlines, the human cost is vast and intimate, and that the least we can do is acknowledge the tragedy and offer practical support.
money_quotes: Both Israelis and Palestinians are crushed right now.
There are no winners in war.
What truly makes me feel desperate and hopeless is the amount of people around the world that are celebrating what happened to us.
It’s not complicated to not kill kids.
Just acknowledging that something horrible has happened (and is happening) is actually really, really important.
key_insight: By centering personal testimonies and concrete ways to help, the post frames the war as a shared human tragedy that demands empathy and practical support, not silence.
